Sully (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $125,070,033Details
International Box Office $118,800,000
Worldwide Box Office $243,870,033
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $15,903,883
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $12,346,056
Total $28,249,939
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On January 15, 2009, the world witnessed the “Miracle on the Hudson” when Captain “Sully” Sullenberger glided his disabled plane onto the frigid waters of the Hudson River, saving the lives of all 155 aboard. However, even as Sully was being heralded by the public and the media for his unprecedented feat of aviation skill, an investigation was unfolding that threatened to destroy his reputation and his career.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$35,028,301 (28.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.57 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,525 opening theaters/3,955 max. theaters, 6.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $172,924,581

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 9th, 2016 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
September 9th, 2016 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 8th, 2016 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Australia)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Slovenia)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Turkey)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide), released as Stebuklas virš Hadsono (Lithuania)
Video Release: December 6th, 2016 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some peril and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2429 (Cert #50318), 6/15/2016)

Synopsis

On January 15, 2009, the world witnessed the “Miracle on the Hudson” when Captain “Sully” Sullenberger glided his disabled plane onto the frigid waters of the Hudson River, saving the lives of all 155 aboard. However, even as Sully was being heralded by the public and the media for his unprecedented feat of aviation skill, an investigation was unfolding that threatened to destroy his reputation and his career.
